title: Ephemerantha celebensis
intro: Ephemerantha celebensis, also known as Celebes orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The Celebes orchid has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of the Celebes orchid include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the Celebes orchid include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
taxonomy: Ephemerantha celebensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of orchid that is native to Sulawesi in Indonesia. Further classification of Ephemerantha celebensis is as follows: it belongs to the genus Ephemerantha and the subfamily Orchidoideae. Within the Orchidoideae subfamily, Ephemerantha celebensis is part of the tribe Coelogyneae. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Orchidaceae family.
